lobbying_activities: 20th May 2025.  CEO invited Minister James Brown to visit St Michael's House - Letter
30 June 2025.  Letter from CEO to list of TD's Ministers advocating for Disability Services in Budget 2026. - Letter

31st July 2025.  CEO and Director of Access, Integration & Adult Services met with Minister Chambers to discuss funding and budgetary concerns for St Michael's House and disability services. - Meeting
June 2025. Official opening of Caislean Day Services.  A day services for adults with disabilities. - Event / Reception

records.specific_details: Invitation to TD's to visit St Michael's House (December 2024)
records.intended_results: Welcoming TD's to visit St Michael's House or have Teams meeting to discuss the challenges within the disability sector and potential solutions.  It is a fantastic opportunity to work together to create positive change for individuals with disabilities and their families and carers.
lobbying_activities: Letters sent to all current TD's in December 2024.  Letter included manifesto produced by National Federation of Voluntary Service Providers. - Letter

records.lobbyist_name: Dun Laoghaire Rathdown Chamber of Commerce
records.specific_details: Support placemaking through Future DLR
records.intended_results: Future DLR and Smart Dún Laoghaire are transforming how the county approaches urban development, using data driven insights to improve sustainability, accessibility, and community life. Budget 2026 should align with these initiatives by funding digital nfrastructure, smart mobility, and innovation-led placemaking that enhances public spaces and supports local businesses.

dpo_entries.public_body: Department of Public Expenditure, NDP Delivery and Reform

lobbying_activities: Town centres in Dún Laoghaire, Blackrock, and Dundrum hold untapped potential to repurpose vacant or underused commercial units—particularly above shops—into housing,coworking spaces, and community hubs. This will reduce vacancy, improve footfall, and foster more vibrant and inclusive urban cores. - Email

records.lobbyist_name: Dun Laoghaire Rathdown Chamber of Commerce
records.specific_details: Support delivery of DLR’s Climate Action Plan
records.intended_results: Dún Laoghaire–Rathdown’s Climate Action Plan setsambitious targets across energy efficiency, transportelectrification, biodiversity, flood resilience, and publicengagement. Budget 2026 should back its implementation through dedicated funding streams that enable local authorities to scale action quickly and visibly. Investment in practical community-led initiatives will help deliver on national targets while building public trust and participation.

dpo_entries.public_body: Department of Public Expenditure, NDP Delivery and Reform

lobbying_activities: DLR is already piloting effective low-carbon solutions, such as solar-powered street furniture and advanced retrofit schemes. With further support, these initiatives can be expanded across towns and neighbourhoods to lower emissions, reduce energypoverty, and support green jobs. Funding should prioritise retrofit grants, microgeneration infrastructure, and electrification of public transport and council fleets. - Lobby day

records.lobbyist_name: Dun Laoghaire Rathdown Chamber of Commerce

Budget 2026 should back its implementation through dedicated funding streams that enable local authorities to scale action quickly and visibly. Investment in practical community-led initiatives will help deliver on national targets while building public trust and participation.

records.lobbyist_name: Dun Laoghaire Rathdown Chamber of Commerce
records.specific_details: Unlocking Housing Supply in Growth Areas
records.intended_results: The Shanganagh Castle development in Shankill - 597 new homes delivered in partnership with the LDA and DLR County Council - sets a strong precedent for affordable, cost-rental, and social housing. Budget 2026 should build on this momentum by accelerating planning and funding for similar large-scale mixed-tenure projects across Dún Laoghaire–Rathdown, particularly in serviced land zones.

dpo_entries.public_body: Department of Public Expenditure, NDP Delivery and Reform

lobbying_activities: To meet urgent housing demand, interim modular housing should be deployed on council-owned landbanks while longterm builds progress. The opening of Woodbrook DART station on 10 August 2025 supports up to 2,300 new homes and must be matched by infrastructure investment and integrated housing delivery to unlock the full potential of the Shankill–Bray corridor. - Email

records.lobbyist_name: Dun Laoghaire Rathdown Chamber of Commerce
records.specific_details: Prioritize DLR SMEs in R&D tax credit expansions
records.intended_results: Many DLR-based SMEs are active in clean tech, biotech, and digital innovation but face barriers accessing R&D tax supports. Budget 2026 should simplify credit access for smaller firms, particularly in areas like Sandyford andCherrywood, where innovation clusters are growing. Thiswould boost local job creation and strengthen Ireland’snational research ecosystem.

records.lobbyist_name: Dun Laoghaire Rathdown Chamber of Commerce
records.specific_details: Support development of a Coastal Innovation District
records.intended_results: Dún Laoghaire–Rathdown is uniquely positioned to host Ireland’s first Coastal Innovation District, building on its access to the sea, harbour infrastructure, and nearby research institutions. Withstrong links to UCD, IADT, and Trinity, the area can drive innovation in blue economy sectors like offshore renewables and marine tech. Budget 2026 should support this vision through targeted investment and public-private partnership incentives.

records.specific_details: Issues and challenges facing sport horse sector
records.intended_results: 1) Provided an update on improvements in HSI horse passporting and registration since 2024. ::2) Highlighted Prof Wall report, horse welfare issues and need for action. ::3) Outlined success of National Breeding Services. ::4) Highlighted high performance programme - successes and gaps. ::5) Explained challenges for the sport horse sector - US tariffs, succession planning, employment permits, skills gaps etc.  ::6) Provided breakdown of HSI structure and funding. ::7) Highlighted challenges facing riding centres around the costs of commercial rates and insurance.

records.specific_details: Increased resources for children's palliative care
records.intended_results: Children's Hospice Week aims:To raise awareness of the broad reaching challenges faced by families caring for a child with palliative care needs, and to highlight the need for a whole-of-government approach to reducing these burdens.::Pre-budget submission aims:1. To increase statutory core funding to €5.3m in 20262. To commence the development of a new national policy on children's palliative care
lobbying_activities: At photoshoot to mark Children's Hospice Week Taoiseach given a summary of recent surveys on the impact of caring for a child with palliative care needs, and urged to support a whole-of-government approach to reducing burdens on families - Event / Reception
Letters re: PBS to Minister for Health, Taoiseach, Tanáiste, Minister for Public Expenditure, and Minister for Finance. - Letter
Meetings to outline LauraLynn Children's Hospice funding situation and to urge TDs to support pre-budget submission asks. - Meeting

records.intended_results: Parliamentary Questions
lobbying_activities: To ask Minister Lawless if a National Training Voucher Scheme will be introduced and funded from the National Training Fund. To ask Minister Lawless if it is now time to extend SUSI funding to QQI  validated programmes at private HEI's. To ask Minister Carroll McNeill if the HSE intends to include the Advanced Health Care Apprenticeship on its list of approved courses as per the Programme for Government’s plan to create career pathways for Health Care Assistants? - Meeting

records.specific_details: Meeting with Maeve O'Connell TD
records.intended_results: Called for supports like Entrepreneurs Relief, Angel Investor Relief, KEEP, and the R&D tax credit to be streamlined to incentivise greater take-up and strengthen Ireland’s indigenous business ecosystem
lobbying_activities: The meeting took place at Leinster House. Dublin Chamber's Competitiveness Report referred to in the meeting - https://www.dublinchamber.ie/Portals/0/Submissions/Competitiveness%20report%20-%20Member%20version%20-%209th%20April%2025%20FV.pdf?ver=R2YMthrK3t3JIL_-4lOjLA%3d%3d - Meeting

records.specific_details: Engagement by representatives from Skillnet Ireland on the upskilling and workforce development supports available, emphasizing the importance of skills in boosting business competitiveness and innovation. Highlighting key areas of proposed expansion in budget 2026 and discussing the utilisation of the National Training Fund.
records.intended_results: Increasing awareness of the supports available through Skillnet Ireland and expanding offerings in 2026.

records.lobbyist_name: Dun Laoghaire Rathdown Chamber of Commerce
records.specific_details: Local Property Tax
records.intended_results: DLR Chamber urges DLR Co Co to take a holistic approach to Housing and retain current LPT levels, and not to increase LPT.  A holistic approach to Housing in DLR must be taken, in terms of planning, affordability and issues such as childcare, as well as increasing revenues coming to DLR Co Co.  This holistic approach must not negatively impact on current DLR citizens who already pay some of the highest property taxes in Ireland, regardless of age profile.
lobbying_activities: DLR Chamber urges DLR Co Co to take a holistic approach to Housing and retain current LPT levels, and not to increase LPT.  A holistic approach to Housing in DLR must be taken, in terms of planning, affordability and issues such as childcare, as well as increasing revenues coming to DLR Co Co.  This holistic approach must not negatively impact on current DLR citizens who already pay some of the highest property taxes in Ireland, regardless of age profile. - Email

records.specific_details: Email concerning the E-liquids Products Tax and concerns around the mechanism chosen to implement the tax via self-declaration rather than as a tax-stamp. Hale also referred to research commissioned from KPMG which raised several important policy considerations around the E-Liquids products tax including the potential for a surge in illicit and grey market sales.
records.intended_results: A review of the proposal to apply an E-liquids tax using self-declaration mechanism rather than a tax stamp.

records.specific_details: Support for Classification of HHC as a Schedule 1 Controlled Drug
records.intended_results: Offer full support from Hale Vaping, Ireland’s largest indigenous vaping company for the proposed Classification of HHC as a Schedule 1 Controlled Drug by the Government. Hale Vaping is not affiliated with the tobacco industry and has always upheld high standards of compliance and public health responsibility. As such, Hale have never stocked HHC products, which Hale view as unregulated and potentially harmful substance.

dpo_entries.public_body: Dáil Éireann, the Lower House of the Oireachtas
Dáil Éireann, the Lower House of the Oireachtas

lobbying_activities: Offer full support from Hale Vaping, Ireland’s largest indigenous vaping company for the proposed Classification of HHC as a Schedule 1 Controlled Drug by the Government. Hale expressed strong support for the proposed legislative action to classify hexahydrocannabinol (HHC) as a Schedule 1 controlled drug under the Misuse of Drugs Act 1977. Hale Vaping is not affiliated with the tobacco industry and has always upheld high standards of compliance and public health responsibility. - Email

lobbying_activities: Email was sent 30th July - Email

records.specific_details: Launch of Samaritans Ireland Impact Report and caller research – which included the following asks:
records.intended_results: •	Prioritise an urgent National Loneliness Strategy within the Taoiseach's office and establish national measurement. •	Mandate Very Large Online Platforms (VLOPs) to ensure content moderator wellbeing through transparent reporting, comprehensive mental health support standards (including contractors), and full inclusion under the Safety, Health and Welfare at Work Act.•	Commit to Samaritans training for all party staff to ensure responsible language on self-harm and suicide

Two meetings held during the relevant period on foot of requests. - Meeting

records.specific_details: Advocacy re Programme for Government
records.intended_results: Secure commitments in Programme for Government to implement joint civil society campaign demands on energy poverty, as well as FoE campaign demands re climate and data centres
lobbying_activities: Email to Minister for Finance's team re FoE programme for government briefing - Email
Email to Taoiseach's team regarding research on data centre and DETE - Email
Emailed joint civil society 'Warm Homes for All' manifesto to FF and FG TDs during programme for government negotiations - Email

records.specific_details: Email to members of the Oireachtas concerning the E-liquids Products Tax and concerns around the mechanism chosen to implement the tax via self-declaration rather than as a tax-stamp. Hale also referred to research commissioned from Forvis Mazars which raised several important policy considerations around the E-Liquids products tax including the potential for a surge in illicit and grey market sales.
records.intended_results: A review of the proposal to apply an E-liquids tax using self-declaration mechanism rather than a tax stamp.

dpo_entries.public_body: Dáil Éireann, the Lower House of the Oireachtas
Dáil Éireann, the Lower House of the Oireachtas

lobbying_activities: Email to members of the Oireachtas referring to the proposed implementation of the E-liquids tax using a self-declaration mechanism rather than a tax stamp and highlighting the findings of an independent report from Forvis Mazars on the matter. - Email

records.specific_details: Activities relating to a briefing session at Leinster House on 25/02/25, where we held a session for all TDs and Senators to present the results of a survey on the experiences of hosts under the pledged accommodation project
records.intended_results: Support for our priorities in this policy area; raising awareness among elected representatives of host experiences
lobbying_activities: Event in Leinster House on 25/02/2025 - Event / Reception

records.specific_details: Correspondence and request for a meeting following election
records.intended_results: Meeting with local TDs/MEPs to introduce Bayer and discuss policy areas of mutual interest.
lobbying_activities: Initial email congratulating on electoral success and requesting a meeting to introduce Bayer. Follow-up email dependent on whether a meeting was to be held. Bayer met with Maria Walsh MEP during the period, discussing a variety of pieces of European legislation, relevant to Bayer's pharmaceutical and crop science businesses where also relevant to the MEP's work. - Email
